data.title: India's Sensex Up 0.3%, Led by Financial, Industrial Stocks — Market Talk
data.description: Indian shares are higher as fears about U.S. tariffs ebb for now. Financial and industrial stocks are leading the gains. Bajaj Finance is up 2.4% and Kotak Mahindra Bank is 1.0% higher. Larsen & Toubro is up 0.8% and NTPC is 1.0% higher. Among the individual movers, Ola Electric Mobility is up 6.4% after saying its sales remain strong and that a temporary backlog in February is being cleared. Investors are focusing on any developments in U.S. trade and foreign policies and their implications for Indian businesses. The benchmark Sensex is up 0.3% at 76566.85. (kosaku.narioka@wsj.com; @kosakunarioka)

data.title: Oil set for second straight weekly gain on Iran sanctions, planned OPEC+ cuts
data.description:
Oil prices rose on Friday, and were set for second consecutive weekly gains, as fresh U.S. sanctions on Iran and a new plan from the Organization of Petroleum Exporting Countries and its allies (OPEC+) to cut output raised bets on tighter supply.

Brent crude futures climbed 21 cents, or 0.3%, to $72.21 per barrel by 0435 GMT. U.S. West Texas Intermediate crude futures were up 25 cents, or 0.4%, to $68.32 a barrel.

On a weekly basis, both Brent and WTI were on track to rise about 2%, their biggest weekly gains since the first week of 2025.

The United States Treasury on Thursday announced new Iran-related sanctions, which for the first time targeted an independent Chinese refiner among other entities and vessels involved in supplying Iranian crude oil to China.

The sanctions on Chinese entities were "a clear escalation in sanctions policy", analysts at RBC Capital Markets said in a note on Friday.

"While the physical implications are minimal, we think it reasonable that risk premium here is taken more seriously," they wrote.

That marked Washington's fourth round of sanctions against Iran since U.S. President Donald Trump in February promised to reimpose a "maximum pressure" campaign on Tehran, pledging to drive the country's oil exports to zero.

Analysts at ANZ Bank said they expect a 1 million barrels per day (bpd) reduction in Iranian crude oil exports because of tighter sanctions. Vessel tracking service Kpler pegged Iranian crude oil exports at over 1.8 million bpd in February.

Oil prices were also supported by a new OPEC+ plan announced Thursday for seven members to further cut output to make up for producing more than agreed levels. The plan would represent monthly cuts of between 189,000 bpd and 435,000 bpd, and will last until June 2026.

OPEC+ earlier this month confirmed that eight of its members would proceed with a monthly increase of 138,000 bpd from April, reversing some of the 5.85 million bpd of output cuts agreed in a series of steps since 2022 to support the market.

"While the group shares a plan for compensation cuts, it certainly doesn't mean members will follow it. A handful of members have consistently produced above their target production levels," ING analysts said in a note on Friday.

data.title: Heathrow Airport Closes Because of Power Outage — WSJ
data.description:
By Hannah Miao

London's Heathrow Airport, a hub for international travel, was shut down for the day on Friday and expects significant disruption in the days to come because of a power outage.

Heathrow will be closed until 11:59 p.m. local time on Friday, according to a statement on the airport's website. Heathrow advised passengers not to travel to the airport and to contact their airlines for more information.

The outage was caused by a fire at an electrical substation supplying the airport, according to Heathrow.

Heathrow doesn't know when power will be reliably restored and will provide an update when more information is available, a spokesperson said.

"We expect significant disruption over the coming days and passengers should not travel to the airport under any circumstances until the airport reopens," the spokesperson said.

The fire broke out late Thursday evening at an electrical substation in western London, according to the London Fire Brigade. Around 70 firefighters were battling the fire, which also caused a power outage affecting many homes and local businesses, the brigade said.

The cause of the fire is currently unknown, according to the brigade.

Friday's closure affects at least 1,300 flights scheduled to and from Heathrow, according to flight tracker Flightradar24.

As of early Friday morning local time, 120 aircraft already in the air would need to divert to alternate airports or return to their origins, according to Flightradar24.

Heathrow was the fourth busiest airport in the world last year ranked by capacity with more than 51.5 million seats, according to data provider OAG. Last year, 83.9 million passengers traveled through Heathrow, according to the airport.

Write to Hannah Miao at hannah.miao@wsj.com

data.title: Chinese Non-Financial Outbound Direct Investments Rise 9% in January-February
data.description:
Chinese non-financial outbound direct investment rose 9.1% to $22.97 billion in January and February, Xinhua News Agency reported Thursday, citing the Commerce Ministry.

Overseas project revenues by Chinese companies slipped 5.6% to $18.3 billion, while the value of new contracts jumped 29% to $35.3 billion, the report said.

In countries covered by the Belt and Road Initiative, non-financial ODI grew 18% to $5.52 billion during the two months combined, the report said.

Contracted project revenues by Chinese firms in Belt and Road countries slipped 5.2% to $15.1 billion, while new contract values increased 34% to $30.9 billion, Xinhua said.

data.title: VSTECS Holdings: Will Continue Executing Strategies to Increase Market Share, Range of Products, Services >0856.HK
Indian bond yields steady as traders await fresh cues
data.description:
Indian government bond yields were little changed in early deals on Friday, as investors awaited fresh triggers after this week's decline.

The benchmark 10-year bond yield (IN067934G=CC) was at 6.6256% as of 10:10 a.m. IST, compared with its previous close of 6.6366%.

"The benchmark yield may gradually shift down towards 6.60% till March end," a trader at a primary dealership said.

"Benign monetary policy outlook, lack of supply with the central bank purchasing debt, likely rate cut and stance change may drive the 10-year yield to 6.55% in April."

The yield on the 10-year note dropped to the lowest level since January 21, 2022, at Thursday's close after the strengthening rupee boosted bets of a rate cut by the Reserve Bank of India next month.

The RBI policy decision is due on April 9. It had cut its key interest rate for the first time in nearly five years in February.

The Indian rupee has advanced more than 1% so far in March, making it a leader among major Asian currency peers.

Investors also cheered the U.S. Federal Reserve's policy decision this week, after it left interest rates unchanged but maintained that it is likely to deliver two quarter-point rate cuts later this year.

Market sentiment was also supported by strong demand during state debt sale and another surprise debt purchase plan announced by the RBI.

The Indian central bank is set to buy bonds worth 500 billion rupees ($5.80 billion) next week, to add to its 1 trillion rupee purchase in March.

Foreign investors have also stepped up purchases of Indian government bonds, with their total investment in bonds that are a part of global indexes crossing 100 billion rupees in March.

These banks net bought bonds worth 119 billion rupees under Fully Accessible Route till March 20, according to clearing house data.

($1 = 86.2675 Indian rupees)

data.title: Nan Yang Dyeing & Finishing Decides Against Paying Dividend
data.description:
Nan Yang Dyeing & Finishing will not distribute dividends, following a board resolution, according to a Thursday Taiwan Exchange filing.

The company, engaged in dyeing and finishing of fiber products, did not disclose further details regarding the decision.

data.title: Fubon Financial Unit Decides Against Dividend Distribution
data.description:
Fubon Financial said Fubon Bank (China) will not distribute dividends, following a board resolution, according to a Thursday Taiwan Exchange filing.

The financial services provider did not disclose further details regarding the decision.

data.title: Offshore Yuan Falls Amid Dollar Strength
data.description:
The offshore yuan depreciated past 7.25 per dollar on Friday, hitting its lowest level in over a week, mainly due to a strengthening US dollar.

The greenback's appreciation was buoyed by remarks from Fed Chair Powell, who indicated that the central bank is in no rush to cut interest rates.

Adding further pressure on the yuan, the People's Bank of China scaled back its support for the currency through its daily midpoint fixings, signaling its intention to curb any sharp or rapid depreciation against the US dollar.

On the monetary policy front, the PBOC kept its key lending rates unchanged for the fifth consecutive month in March 2025, in line with market expectations.

The one-year loan prime rate remained at 3.1%, while the five-year loan prime rate held steady at 3.6%.

Both rates are at historic lows, following cuts in October and July of 2024.

Still, the PBOC remarked that it will lower interest rates and adjust the bank’s reserve requirement ratio at the appropriate time.

data.title: Zhaojin Mining's 2024 Profit Zooms 111%
data.description:
Zhaojin Mining Industry's attributable profit surged 111% to 1.45 billion yuan in 2024 from 686.4 million yuan in 2023, according to a Friday filing with the Hong Kong bourse.

Earnings per share at the mining company increased 150% to 0.35 yuan from 0.14 yuan a year earlier. Analysts at Visible Alpha estimated EPS at 0.43 yuan.

Revenue grew 37% to 11.6 billion yuan from 8.42 billion yuan in the previous year. Visible Alpha analysts forecast revenue at 10.3 billion yuan.

The company recommended a final dividend of 0.05 yuan per share, up from 0.04 yuan per share in the previous year. The dividend is subject to shareholder approval.

Shares fell 3% during Friday's late morning trading.

data.title: China Resources Beverage's Attributable Profit Inches Up in 2024
data.description:
China Resources Beverage's attributable profit rose to 1.64 billion yuan in 2024 from 1.33 billion yuan in 2023, according to a Friday filing with the Hong Kong bourse.

Earnings per share at the beverage company reached 0.79 yuan. Analysts at Visible Alpha estimated EPS at 0.71 yuan.

Revenue stayed flat at 13.5 billion yuan. Visible Alpha analysts forecast revenue at 14.1 billion yuan.

The company declared a final dividend of 0.307 yuan per share and a special dividend of 0.176 yuan per share, both payable July 24 to shareholders on record as of June 16.

data.title: Cathay Pacific's Passenger Count Rises 14% in February
data.description:
Cathay Pacific Airways flew 2.1 million passengers in February, a 14.3% increase from a year ago.

Passenger capacity rose 17% to 10.2 billion available seat kilometers (ASK), according to a Thursday filing with the Hong Kong bourse.

Cargo carried increased 12% to 119,707 tons, while cargo capacity grew 3.5% to 1.11 billion available freight-tonne kilometers.

Subsidiary HK Express carried 585,941 passengers, a 32% increase year over year.

data.title: ProSiebenSat.1 Media Approves Purchase from General Atlantic of NuCom, ParshipMeet Interests
data.description:
ProSiebenSat.1 Media said Thursday its executive and supervisory boards consented to the proposed purchase from General Atlantic of minority interests in the omnichannel platform Nucom Group and online dating platform ParshipMeet Group.

The German mass media and digital company will pay 10 million euros in cash and issue 5.9 million treasury shares or a 2.5% stake as consideration for the transaction. Meanwhile, General Atlantic also agreed to a fixed exit participation of 50 million euros after ProSiebenSat.1's exit from ParshipMeet. The deal to formalize the transaction is expected to be signed shortly.

The purchase is subject to the completion of the sale of price comparison website Verivox, which was approved the same day. After the transaction, ProSiebenSat.1 will gain full control over NuCom and ParshipMeet, as well as perfume e-retailer Flaconi, which will be 28.4% owned by General Atlantic.

data.title: Al Fujairah National Insurance to Halt Trading on Abu Dhabi Bourse
data.description:
Al Fujairah National Insurance Co. (ADX:AFNIC) shares will temporarily halt trading on the Abu Dhabi Securities Exchange from 12 pm local time on April 14 due to its annual general meeting.

The Abu Dhabi-listed insurer will resume trading after it publishes the results of its meeting, according to a Thursday release.

data.title: Nuvama initiates coverage on Waaree Energies with a Buy, target of Rs 2,805
data.description:
Nuvama Institutional Equities has initiated coverage on Waaree Energies with a 'Buy' rating with a target price of Rs 2,805 per share, citing strong growth prospects driven by capacity expansion and integration into new energy segments.

The shares of Waaree Energies are trading at Rs 11.75 as of 9:52 AM, on March 21, higher by 0.52 percent.

The brokerage projects a 30 percent revenue CAGR and 54 percent EBITDA CAGR for FY24–27, supported by a surge in module, cell, and wafer capacity. Waaree's margins are expected to peak at 24 percent by FY28, before moderating due to rising competition and lower import tariffs.

The company is expanding into green hydrogen, electrolysers, lithium-ion cells, inverters, and battery energy storage systems (BESS) to diversify operations and sustain long-term growth. Nuvama likens the current phase of India’s renewable energy sector to the early IT boom of the 1990s, suggesting a multi-decadal opportunity.

On valuation, Waaree’s 24x FY25 estimated EV/EBITDA reflects its rapid growth trajectory, with expectations of a correction to 11x by FY27 estimated as earnings catch up. However, risks remain, including potential changes in import tariffs, evolving technology, and oversupply concerns in the module segment.

Waaree Energies' EBITDA margin is expected to rise from 14% in FY24 to 23% by FY27, driven by backward integration and a shift toward higher-margin DCR modules. Its solar module, cell, and wafer capacities are set to expand to 21GW, 11GW, and 6GW, strengthening competitiveness. Strong cash flows are expected to support capex and investments in allied areas, while its horizontal and vertical integration strategy aims to sustain long-term growth and de-risk the business.Disclaimer: The views and investment tips expressed by experts on Moneycontrol.com are their own and not those of the website or its management. Moneycontrol.com advises users to check with certified experts before taking any investment decisions.

Texmaco Rail & Engineering Makes Additional Investment in UAE-Based Subsidiary; Shares Rise 5%
data.description:
Texmaco Rail & Engineering has invested an undisclosed amount into its UAE-based wholly-owned subsidiary Texmaco Middle East, according to a Thursday filing to the Indian stock exchanges.

Texmaco Middle East trades in trains spare parts and components, crane rails and fixing accessories, heavy equipment and machinery spare parts, simulation equipment and systems, among others.

The investment will help the subsidiary to explore additional opportunities in sectors to supplement the businesses of the parent company.

Shares of the company were up nearly 5% in recent trade.

data.title: China, HK stocks head for weekly loss on tech sell-off
data.description:
China and Hong Kong stocks declined on Friday and were headed for a weekly loss, as tech shares tumbled on mounting profit-taking pressure.

** At the midday break, the Shanghai Composite index was down 0.9% and China's blue-chip CSI300 index weakened 1.11%. Both indexes were headed for their biggest weekly declines since February.

** Hong Kong's benchmark Hang Seng Index was down 2%, pulling back further from three-year high.

** The tech sector led declines in both onshore and offshore markets. Hong Kong's Hang Seng Tech index tumbled 2.9%, while mainland's tech-focused Star 50 Index dropped 2% and AI-related shares slipped 2.7%.

** Chipmaker Semiconductor Manufacturing International Corporation slid 6.7% to a one-month low, while Alibaba (998.HK) lost 3.3%.

** Major indexes are showing increased downside momentum on declining volume, indicating further weakness, analysts at Caida Securities said on Friday.

** "The market may need to work through this corrective phase before resuming its broader upward trajectory."

** The Hang Seng Tech index has lost 3.7% so far this week in a second week of decline - the longest losing streak since the opening weeks of the year.

** However, the gauge is still up 27% year-to-date.

data.title: Market Chatter: Niger Expels Three Chinese Oil Executives Over Pay Disparities
data.description:
Niger expelled three Chinese oil executives over wage disparities between expatriate and local workers, Reuters reported Thursday, citing Niger's Oil Minister Sahabi Oumarou announced.

The officials, linked to China National Petroleum Corporation (CNPC), West African Oil Pipeline Company (WAPCo), and SORAZ refinery, were ordered to leave within 48 hours, Reuter said.

Oumarou cited unequal salaries, with Chinese employees earning $8,678 per month, compared to $1,200 for Nigeriens in the same roles. China and Niger have partnered been working on oil fields in East Niger since 2008, the publication said.

(Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.)

data.title: Correction to Heathrow Article
data.description: Heathrow Airport handled 120,765 metric tons of cargo in February. "Heathrow Airport Will Be Closed Friday Due to Power Outage — Update," at 0335 GMT, incorrectly said the airport handled 120.77 metric tons of cargo.

data.title: African Markets - Factors to watch on March 21
data.description:
The following company announcements, scheduled economic indicators, debt and currency market moves and political events may affect African markets on Friday.

- - - - -

GLOBAL MARKETS

Asian stocks inched lower on Friday in a subdued end to the week as deepening geopolitical worries and fears over U.S. tariffs and their impact on the global economy curbed investors appetite for risk, keeping safe-haven gold near record highs.

WORLD OIL PRICES

Oil prices rose in early Asian trading on Friday, and were set for their second consecutive weekly gains, after fresh U.S. sanctions on Iran and a new OPEC+ plan for seven members to cut output raised bets on tightening supply.

EMERGING MARKETS

For the top emerging markets news, double click on

AFRICA STOCKS

For the latest news on South African stocks, click on

SOUTH AFRICA MARKETS

South Africa's rand remained on the back foot on Thursday after the country's central bank left its main interest rate unchanged as expected.

KENYA MARKETS

The Kenyan shilling held steady on Thursday compared with the previous session, supported by subdued dollar demand from importers, traders said.

NIGERIA POLITICS

Nigerian lawmakers approved on Thursday President Bola Tinubu's state of emergency measures and suspension of an opposition governor in oil-producing Rivers state in the Niger Delta region.

DEMOCRATIC REPUBLIC OF CONGO WAR

The leader of Rwandan-backed M23 rebels in eastern Congo said on Thursday that a call by Kinshasa and Kigali for an immediate ceasefire "doesn't concern us" as his forces pushed deeper into Congolese territory by capturing the strategic town of Walikale.

TANZANIA-ZAMBIA RAILWAY

The China Civil Engineering Construction Corporation (CCECC) will invest $1.4 billion to upgrade the Tanzania-Zambia railway, the line's operator said on Thursday, improving a key route for copper exports from central Africa.

ETHIOPIA-ERITREA TENSIONS

Ethiopia's Prime Minister Abiy Ahmed said on Thursday that his government would not seek conflict with longtime foe Eritrea over access to the Red Sea, after regional officials and experts warned of a possible war between the Horn of Africa neighbours.

SOUTH SUDAN POLITICS

South Sudan's President Salva Kiir has sacked the governor of northeastern Upper Nile state where clashes have escalated between government troops and an ethnic militia he accuses of allying with his rival and First Vice President Riek Machar.

data.title: UOB-Kay Hian Purchases Additional Shares in Thai Subsidiary
data.description:
UOB-Kay Hian , through its unit UOB-Kay Hian (BVI), acquired 75,000 additional shares in its Thai subsidiary, UOB Kay Hian Securities Thailand (SET:UOBKH), at 4.9406 baht apiece.

Following the purchase, the total number of shares held directly by UOB-Kay Hian in the Thai entity remains unchanged at 70.65%, according to a Thursday filing with the Singapore Exchange.

data.title: New York Times business news - March 21
data.description:
The following are the top stories on the New York Times business pages. Reuters has not verified these stories and does not vouch for their accuracy.

- Tesla is recalling nearly all Cybertrucks in the United States to fix an exterior panel that could detach while driving, the company said on Thursday, the latest in a series of call-backs for the pickup truck.

- Billionaire Elon Musk, U.S. President Donald Trump's close ally, is due to be briefed on Friday by the Pentagon on the U.S. military's plan for any war that might break out with China.

- The Federal Housing Finance Agency, now under the direction of William Pulte, placed 35 unionized employees on administrative leave over the past two days.

- A federal judge on Thursday blocked Elon Musk's Department of Government Efficiency from accessing Social Security records as part of its hunt under President Donald Trump for fraud and waste, calling the effort a "fishing expedition."

data.title: Keppel Infrastructure Trust Completes Sale of 50% Stake in Philippine Coastal Storage
data.description:
Keppel Infrastructure Trust completed the sale of its 50% stake in Philippine Coastal Storage and Pipeline, according to a Thursday filing with the Singapore Exchange.

The trust, along with Metro Pacific Investments, sold their combined 100% stake in Philippine Coastal Storage to affiliates of I Squared Capital for $460 million.

data.title: China Resources Beverage 2024 Net Rose on Higher Revenue, Lower Finance Costs
data.description:
(04:12 GMT) *China Resources Beverage 2024 Net CNY1.64B Vs. Net CNY1.33B >2460.HK
(04:32 GMT) *China Resources Beverage 2024 Rev CNY13.52B Vs. CNY13.51B >2460.HK
(04:41 GMT) China Resources Beverage 2024 Net Rose on Higher Revenue, Lower Finance Costs

By Amanda Lee

China Resources Beverage's net profit rose in 2024, thanks to higher revenue in its beverage products segment and lower finance costs.

Net profit rose 23% to 1.64 billion yuan, equivalent to US$226.3 million, compared with a year earlier, the ready-to-drink soft beverage company said Friday.

The company's full-year revenue rose 0.05% to 13.52 billion yuan.

Revenue for its beverage products segment rose 31% to 1.40 billion yuan.

Lower finance costs also supported its results, falling 9.4% compared with a year ago to 2.4 million yuan. This was due to a decrease in bank borrowings during the year, the company said.

It proposed a final dividend of CNY0.307 a share for 2024 and a special dividend of CNY0.176 a share.

Write to Amanda Lee at amanda.lee@wsj.com
